candidates.name: California Air Resources Board (CARB)
candidates.rationale: Primary CA regulator for freight emissions: Truck & Bus Regulation, Advanced Clean Trucks, Advanced Clean Fleets, drayage ZEV mandates, TRU rules, Low Carbon Fuel Standard, cap-and-trade.

candidates.name: California Air Resources Board – Laws and Regulations Portal
candidates.rationale: Canonical sub-portal publishing CARB's CCR Title 13/17 regulations, executive orders, district rules, and rulemaking history directly affecting heavy-duty freight vehicles.

candidates.name: California Environmental Protection Agency (CalEPA)
candidates.rationale: Cabinet-level parent of CARB, SWRCB, DTSC, and OEHHA; coordinates statewide environmental enforcement including freight-related air, water, and hazmat programs.

candidates.name: California Energy Commission (CEC)
candidates.rationale: Administers Clean Transportation Program and Low Carbon Fuel Production Program; sets ZEV infrastructure policy and LCFS-adjacent fuel standards affecting freight operators.

candidates.name: California Energy Commission – Fuels and Transportation Division
candidates.rationale: Canonical sub-portal for CEC's freight-relevant programs: ZEV infrastructure funding, Clean Transportation Program grants for MHD vehicles, hydrogen fueling, and LCFS fuel production.

candidates.name: California Office of Administrative Law (OAL) – California Regulatory Notice Register & CCR
candidates.rationale: Publishes the official California Code of Regulations and weekly Regulatory Notice Register—the canonical gazette where all CARB, CEC, and other freight-relevant rulemakings are formally noticed.

candidates.name: California State Water Resources Control Board (SWRCB)
candidates.rationale: Issues Industrial General Permit (NPDES stormwater) covering trucking terminals, warehouses, ports, and railyards; also regulates ballast water and port facility runoff.

candidates.name: California Highway Patrol (CHP) – Commercial Vehicle Section
candidates.rationale: Canonical CA regulator for commercial vehicle safety, HOS, weight/size limits, BIT terminal inspections, motor carrier CA-number registration, and intrastate ELD rules.

candidates.name: California Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) – Motor Carrier Permits
candidates.rationale: Issues California Motor Carrier Permits (MCP) required for freight operators on CA highways; manages CARB registration linkage and Employer Pull Notice program for driver records.

candidates.name: California Department of Industrial Relations – Cal/OSHA (Division of Occupational Safety and Health)
candidates.rationale: Canonical CA labor/safety regulator for freight workers; enforces CCR Title 8 standards covering warehouse operations, heat illness, forklift safety, AB 701 warehouse quotas, and hazmat handling.

candidates.name: California Public Utilities Commission (CPUC)
candidates.rationale: Regulates freight railroad safety, intrastate rail carriers, and Clean Miles Standard for transport fleets; also sets GHG/ZEV electrification requirements for passenger and common carriers.

candidates.name: South Coast Air Quality Management District (SCAQMD)
candidates.rationale: Largest CA local air district; issues rules directly affecting freight fleets in LA/OC/SB/Riverside counties—including Rule 2305 (Warehouse Indirect Source Rule) and fleet electrification mandates.

candidates.name: Bay Area Air Quality Management District (BAAQMD)
candidates.rationale: Issues freight-relevant stationary and mobile source rules for the nine-county Bay Area, including port and railyard indirect source and idling regulations supplementing CARB rules.

candidates.name: San Joaquin Valley Air Pollution Control District (SJVAPCD)
candidates.rationale: Issues fleet rules and Indirect Source Review (Rule 9510) covering warehouses and distribution centers in the Central Valley; supplements CARB truck/bus regulation enforcement.

candidates.name: California Department of Transportation (Caltrans)
candidates.rationale: Regulates legal truck access, size/weight permits, freight corridor planning, and sustainable freight infrastructure policy including ZEV freight route planning under SB 1.

candidates.name: California State Transportation Agency (CalSTA)
candidates.rationale: Cabinet agency overseeing Caltrans, CPUC rail, DMV, CHP, and port-related freight policy coordination; issues state freight policy guidance and sustainable freight action plan.

candidates.name: California Legislature – Legislative Information (leginfo)
candidates.rationale: Official source for California statutes (Health & Safety Code, Vehicle Code, Public Resources Code) underlying all freight-sustainability mandates including AB 32, SB 375, AB 2061.

candidates.name: Port of Los Angeles – Environmental Programs
candidates.rationale: Largest US container port; publishes Clean Air Action Plan (CAAP), Clean Truck Program, and Shore Power mandates—primary source for drayage ZEV and port-freight emissions requirements.

candidates.name: Port of Long Beach – Environmental Stewardship
candidates.rationale: Co-publishes Clean Air Action Plan with POLA; issues independent Green Port Policy, tariff-embedded clean truck and vessel speed reduction requirements affecting freight operators.

candidates.name: California Air Resources Board – Low Carbon Fuel Standard (LCFS) Program
candidates.rationale: Canonical sub-portal for LCFS—California's primary fuel carbon-intensity mandate covering diesel, biodiesel, renewable natural gas, hydrogen, and electricity used in freight transport.

results.name: California Air Resources Board (CARB)
results.rationale: CARB is California's canonical air-quality regulator with direct freight impact via truck/bus regulation, ZEV standards, emissions caps, and heavy-duty vehicle programs.

results.name: California Air Resources Board – Laws and Regulations Portal
results.rationale: CARB canonical regulator; air quality laws directly affect truck emissions & port ops; Title 13 motor vehicle regs operationally impact freight fleets.

results.name: California Environmental Protection Agency (CalEPA)
results.rationale: CalEPA is canonical CA environmental regulator. Umbrella agency housing CARB, DTSC, SWRCB—all with freight-material mandates (emissions, hazmat, water). Primary T2 source.

results.name: California Energy Commission (CEC)
results.rationale: CEC is CA's primary energy agency; canonical regulator of appliance/building efficiency, clean transportation, power plants, renewables. Clean Transportation Program directly affects freight electrifi

results.name: California Energy Commission – Fuels and Transportation Division
results.rationale: CEC Fuels & Transportation Division — canonical state regulator of vehicle standards, alternative fuels, ZEV infrastructure, low-carbon fuel production. Directly operationalizes freight via heavy-duty

results.name: California Office of Administrative Law (OAL) – California Regulatory Notice Register & CCR
results.rationale: California's canonical regulatory publication & codification authority. Publishes CCR & Notice Register where all state agency regulations—including freight, emissions, energy, vehicle standards—are o

results.name: California State Water Resources Control Board (SWRCB)
results.rationale: Primary water regulator with sustainability mandate. Indirect freight impact via water quality, groundwater, agricultural discharge permitting affecting supply chains.

results.name: California Highway Patrol (CHP) – Commercial Vehicle Section
results.rationale: CHP Commercial Vehicle Section is canonical CA regulator for motor carrier safety, vehicle registration, hazmat transport, and commercial fleet compliance—directly operationally affecting freight.

results.name: California Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) – Motor Carrier Permits
results.rationale: CA DMV motor carrier permits directly regulate commercial freight operations, fleet compliance, hazmat transport. Primary state regulator of carrier registration and vehicle standards affecting freigh

results.name: California Department of Industrial Relations – Cal/OSHA (Division of Occupational Safety and Health)
results.rationale: Cal/OSHA is canonical state regulator (T2) but primary mandate is occupational safety/health, not sustainability/climate/environmental. Indirect freight relevance via driver/warehouse worker safety on

results.name: California Public Utilities Commission (CPUC)
results.rationale: Canonical CA utility regulator. Direct freight impact: electric vehicle charging, port electrification, clean miles standard, energy/fuel regulation. Primary regulatory publisher.

results.name: South Coast Air Quality Management District (SCAQMD)
results.rationale: SCAQMD is canonical air-quality regulator for South Coast CA. Emissions standards, permitting, & INVEST CLEAN zero-emission goods-movement program directly operationalize freight compliance.

results.name: Bay Area Air Quality Management District (BAAQMD)
results.rationale: BAAQMD is canonical regional air-quality regulator under CA delegation. Rules, permits, emissions standards directly operationalize freight via mobile/portable equipment, refinery rules, clean transpo

results.name: San Joaquin Valley Air Pollution Control District (SJVAPCD)
results.rationale: SJVAPCD is canonical air-quality regulator with direct freight impact via emissions standards, equipment rules, diesel equipment incentive programs affecting commercial fleets & equipment.

results.name: California Department of Transportation (Caltrans)
results.rationale: Caltrans is CA's canonical transport authority; homepage shows freight investment & mass transit programs but lacks detailed regulatory content.

results.name: California State Transportation Agency (CalSTA)
results.rationale: CalSTA is canonical CA transport regulator overseeing Caltrans, CHP, DMV, HSR. Manages roads, trains, water transport. Direct operational impact on freight via vehicle standards, infrastructure, safet

results.name: California Legislature – Legislative Information (leginfo)
results.rationale: Canonical primary legislative archive hosting all CA bills & codes. Hosts freight-affecting SB253, SB261, AB1305, vehicle, labor, energy regulations.

results.name: Port of Los Angeles – Environmental Programs
results.rationale: Port authority primary source on air quality, emissions, alternative fuels, water quality, sustainability—direct operational impact on cargo, vessels, trucks, warehousing. Canonical regulator for LA p

results.name: Port of Long Beach – Environmental Stewardship
results.rationale: Port of Long Beach is canonical California port authority with direct operational control over major freight/cargo hub. Environmental stewardship page governs air quality, emissions, vessel operations

results.name: California Air Resources Board – Low Carbon Fuel Standard (LCFS) Program
results.rationale: CARB canonical regulator; LCFS directly regulates fuel carbon intensity for transportation fuels, operationally impacting freight fuel costs and alternative-fuel adoption.

known_irrelevant.name: Los Angeles County Museum of Art
